instruction:
Let $p$ be a prime number and denote by $\mathbb{F}_{p}$ the field with $p$ elements. Consider the groups $G=\mathrm{G L}_{2} ( \mathbb{F}_{p} )$ . and $G^{\prime}=\mathrm{P G L}_{2} ( \mathbb{F}_{p} )=G / \mathbb{F}_{p}^{\times}$ , where the group of units in $\mathbb{F}_{p}$ embeds diagonally into $G$ .\
What is the value of $| G |$ and $| G^{\prime} |$ ?

Options:
A. $$
|G|=p(p-1)^2(p),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p+2)
$$
B. $$
|G|=p^2(p-1)^2,|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p-1)
$$
C. $$
|G|=p(p-1)^2(p+2),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p)
$$
D. $$
|G|=p(p+1)^2(p-1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p)(p+1)
$$
E. $$
|G|=p^2(p-1)(p+1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-2)(p+1)
$$
F. $$
|G|=p(p-2)^2(p+1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p+3)
$$
G. $$
|G|=p(p-1)^2(p-1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p-2)
$$
H. $$
|G|=p(p-1)^3(p+1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p+3)
$$
I. $$
|G|=p(p-1)^2(p+1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p-1)(p+1)
$$
J. $$
|G|=p(p-1)^2(p+1),|G^{\prime}|=p(p+1)(p-1)
$$
